title: Corpus testing and basic differential testing
description:
Build the differential testing infrastructure to validate correctness against C tree-sitter for grammars without external scanners.

This is the early testing bead — it validates the code generator output and core parser correctness before external scanners are integrated. Uses the corpus test runner (already built in an earlier bead) as foundation.

Deliverables:
- Differential testing harness: parse corpus files with both C tree-sitter (via CLI invocation or CGo test helper) and Go implementation. Compare S-expression output.
- Run tree-sitter test corpus (test/corpus/ in each grammar repo) for JSON, Go (basic — no external scanner tokens), and any grammar generated by agm.
- Node-level comparison: compare byte ranges, point ranges, child counts, and field assignments (not just S-expressions).
- Real-world corpus collection: version-pinned files from popular repos (Go stdlib, npm package.json files, etc.) in testdata/corpora/.
- Regression test directory: testdata/regressions/ with input + .expected pairs for any divergences found.
- CI integration: run corpus tests on every commit.

Tests:
- The framework itself validates against C tree-sitter
- Regression suite never shrinks

Reference: docs/testing-strategy.md §2, §4, docs/design.md §10 Risk: Parse Tree Compatibility
